_comment: Choice cards for tone: ghost. Each card belongs to a trait, filtered by god domain. The Warden is a keeper of the dead, a witness, a threshold guardian. Choices reflect that weight.

authority.text: Name yourself Warden-Sovereign and read the compact aloud.
authority.subtext: The words were sealed for a reason. Unsealing them is a kind of crowning.

authority.text: Insist the compact remains valid even without living witnesses.
authority.subtext: A law is not a law only while someone watches. You will hold it regardless.

authority.text: Bar the hall door and stand before it. This place is not surrendered.
authority.subtext: You do not fight the god. You simply refuse to move.

authority.text: Show the god the lock on the chest. Tell it: this is not yours to open.
authority.subtext: The craftsman understands ownership. A made thing belongs to its maker's intent.

wisdom.text: Ask the god what the names meant before the war renamed them.
wisdom.subtext: The dead had other names once. Knowing them might change what you owe.

wisdom.text: Ask why the torch burns that color. Listen to the whole answer.
wisdom.subtext: You have been watching this flame for seven winters. Tonight it speaks. Pay attention.

wisdom.text: Ask the goddess what the dead in this keep are still waiting for.
wisdom.subtext: She moves between worlds. She knows what the dead cannot say for themselves.

wisdom.text: Ask what this keep was before it was a fortress. Before the first stone was cut.
wisdom.subtext: The land remembers what the builders forgot. There was something here before the war.

wisdom.text: Ask what the sea has taken from this cliff that you were supposed to keep.
wisdom.subtext: You know the answer. You need the god to confirm it before you can act.

courage.text: Tell the god plainly: I am afraid of you, and I am staying anyway.
courage.subtext: There is no point pretending. The god of war can smell pretense.

courage.text: Open the chest in front of the goddess and read every name aloud.
courage.subtext: You were told to guard the names. You were not told to hide them from the dead's own queen.

courage.text: Go to the battlements in the storm and call the goddess by name. Let her come to you.
courage.subtext: You stop waiting to be found. You do the finding.

courage.text: Stand at the cliff edge while the god speaks. Do not step back.
courage.subtext: The sea god tests with height. You answer with stillness.

courage.text: Look the Thunderer in the eye and say: I have already decided.
courage.subtext: A decision made in private, held through divine pressure, is the most costly kind of courage.

cunning.text: Give the god the names of the horses. Say nothing else.
cunning.subtext: Hermes trades in names. He did not specify whose names. Horses remember better anyway.

cunning.text: Tell the goddess the keep is beautiful, especially in the storm. Watch her pause.
cunning.subtext: Aphrodite is never told she has overlooked something lovely. The delay is yours to use.

cunning.text: Ask Athena what the god who visited last night wanted. Frame it as curiosity.
cunning.subtext: The gods talk among themselves. You are gathering intelligence.

cunning.text: Offer the Thunderer something he didn't ask for. Let him believe it was his idea to want it.
cunning.subtext: Kings are easy to manage when they feel magnanimous. Gods are kings.

cunning.text: Ask the craftsman to repair the lock on the chest. While he works, move the contents.
cunning.subtext: Hephaestus is absorbed by the problem in front of him. That is his gift and your opportunity.

devotion.text: Give the goddess every name freely. They belong to her people, not to your compact.
devotion.subtext: The dead are hers. You were only holding them until someone with the right came to collect.

devotion.text: Carry the sealed chest to the cliff edge and release it to the storm.
devotion.subtext: What the sea made, the sea can keep. You are done being the one who holds it.

devotion.text: Speak the names into the strange-colored flame and watch them go.
devotion.subtext: Light carries. The names will reach whoever needs to hear them.

devotion.text: Acknowledge the compact is dissolved. The signatories are dead. The terms died with them.
devotion.subtext: Hera understands that oaths outlive their makers only as long as someone chooses to honor them. You choose otherwise.

devotion.text: Ask the goddess to carry word to the dead that they have been witnessed. Let the hunt be finished.
devotion.subtext: Some things pursued for seven winters can be set down, if you can find the right messenger.
